evidence_quotes.quote: "unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns"
video game publishers having a "Buy the game" button, as exists for instance in Ubisoft Connect software
"You buy a \"license\" to play the game for an unspecified amount of time."
"Ubisoft reserves the right to suspend or close Your account"

paragraphs.text: (This piece of feedback will be centered around Ubisoft but that's just what I'm most familiar with. I know there are other platforms, if not all of them, that have the same issue) The Digital Fairness Act lists one of the aims as "unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns". This I think should also cover video game publishers having a "Buy the game" button, as exists for instance in Ubisoft Connect software, when in reality Ubisoft can unilaterally shut down the game servers (at least on certain games) and You will have no way of playing the game anymore. Instead of "Buying the Game" You buy a "license" to play the game for an unspecified amount of time. In addition to shutting down the game servers, Ubisoft reserves the right to suspend or close Your account (https://www.ubisoft.com/legal/documents/termsofuse/en-INTL 6.9) I strongly believe that if I'm not going to own the game after clicking "Buy the game" button (or even just "Buy") then that is a misleading practice / a dark pattern. I further believe that publishers shouldn't be able to just take the thing You bought away (even if it is just a license) whenever they want to but that might be out of scope for the DFA, but hopefully the EU Citizens Initiative Stop Destroying Videogames will be successful and cover that. The Digital Fairness Act seems like a good idea and I hope it will not leave people who play video games behind. Thank You for reading
